title: aconitum × vulgare
intro: Aconitum × vulgare, also known as Monk's Hood or Wolf's Bane, is a hybrid plant in the Aconitum genus that is native to Europe and Asia. The plant contains toxic compounds, particularly aconitine, which has been used in traditional medicine for its analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties. Aconitum × vulgare has been used to treat various health issues, including pain, fever, and respiratory problems, and is believed to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health. The plant is typically prepared as a tincture, infusion, or ointment, although its use should be approached with caution due to its toxicity and potential side effects.
